summary: In dentistry, when an interdigital papilla protrudes through a rubber dam, it commonly occurs due to improperly placed punches that are positioned too closely together. This leads to insufficient space between them, causing tissue displacement and exposure of the papillae during treatment.

This leads to insufficient space between them, causing tissue displacement and exposure of the papillae during treatment.. Answer choice: D
